entity.description: Face of God, father of the prophetess Anna (q.v.), Luke 2:36.

nodes.label: Phanuel
nodes.description: Face of God, father of the prophetess Anna (q.v.), Luke 2:36.

nodes.label: Anna
nodes.description: Grace, an aged widow, the daughter of Phanuel. She was a “prophetess,” like Miriam, Deborah, and Huldah (2 Chr. 34:22). After seven years of married life her husband died, and during her long widowhood she daily attended the temple services. When she was eighty-four years old, she entered the temple at the moment when the aged Simeon uttered his memorable words of praise and thanks to God that he had fulfilled his ancient promise in sending his Son into the world (Luke 2:36, 37).

verses.text: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;
